title: Portuguese Dough Fritters
description: A crispy and delicious dessert originating from Portugal. Made with a simple dough and topped with a sweet glaze.

ingredients.name: Dough
ingredients.notes: 1 cup warm water, 3 tablespoons sugar, 4 cups all-purpose flour

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine yeast, warm water, and sugar. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes until yeast is activated.
Add flour to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan to 375°F (190°C).
Once the oil is hot, add the cubed bread and fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the bread from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a small bowl, mix together the sugar and water to make the glaze.
Drizzle the glaze over the fried bread and serve immediately.
